summary.health_score_note: Weighted average of 7 scored on-site categories (Technical, Content, Schema, Sitemap, Visual/UX, GEO, E-commerce). Performance audit still running at report time — excluded, will shift the score once in. SXO (34) and Backlinks (8) are shown separately as diagnostic/off-page context, not folded into this number.
summary.business_type: E-commerce — industrial equipment reseller (shipping containers, refrigeration units, tanks, generators, trailers/chassis), direct-to-buyer, ~212 SKUs across 8 categories
top_findings: Three separate critical bugs all traced back to the same root causes and were fixed live during this audit: (1) Vercel image-optimizer 402 broke every product photo site-wide, (2) a JSON-LD image-URL concatenation bug broke Product schema images on all 212 products, (3) ExpandableText truncated descriptions to 480 chars server-side, hiding ~67% of every rewritten description from crawlers.

Product photos are hotlinked directly from third-party domains (conexdepotshipping.com, cdn.shopify.com) rather than self-hosted — flagged independently by three specialists (visual, e-commerce, SXO) as a reliability, trust, and duplicate-content risk.

A near-identical competitor brand, "Container One" (containerone.net — 30+ years, BBB-accredited, 300+ locations), already ranks page-1 for shared target queries. This is a brand-collision risk independent of any on-site fix.
No FAQ content or FAQPage schema anywhere on the site — the single highest-leverage gap for AI Overview/ChatGPT citation (GEO score 58/100).
About/Contact pages and the 4-post blog carry very thin E-E-A-T trust signal for a high-ticket industrial equipment seller (no address, no bylines, no credentials).
quick_wins: Add security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.) via next.config.ts headers() — none exist today
Populate sitemap <lastmod> sitewide (currently only 4 of 229 URLs have it)
Add a 3-5 item FAQ block + FAQPage schema per product template

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Clean robots.txt, correct canonicals, HSTS sitewide, no mixed content
Full SSR/prerendering — no JS dependency for core content or JSON-LD
Clean hyphenated URL structure, working legacy-slug redirects
Sitemap: 229 URLs, zero duplicates, all spot-checked URLs return 200

findings.title: No security headers beyond HSTS
findings.description: No CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, or Permissions-Policy on any page. next.config.ts has no headers() block.
findings.recommendation: Add a headers() block in next.config.ts with baseline security headers scoped to the Vercel/Shopify hosts in use.

findings.title: Category hub pages are fully dynamic, uncached
findings.description: Unlike home/product pages (prerendered, edge-cached), the 8 category pages serve Cache-Control: private, no-store with no X-Nextjs-Prerender header — rendered fresh every request. These are the highest link-equity internal hubs.
findings.recommendation: Convert to static generation with ISR (revalidate) or find and remove the dynamic data source forcing per-request rendering.

findings.title: Sitemap lastmod present on only 4 of 229 URLs
findings.description: Only the 4 blog posts carry lastmod; every product/category/static page is missing it, limiting crawl-budget prioritization for a young domain.
findings.recommendation: Populate lastmod from real content-modified timestamps sitewide, not a blanket 'now' stamp.

findings.title: Two-hop redirect for bare-HTTP apex
findings.description: http://containeronedepot.com takes 2 redirect hops to reach the canonical https://www. host.
findings.recommendation: Collapse to a single redirect at the DNS/edge layer.

findings.title: No IndexNow integration
findings.description: No IndexNow key file found; relying solely on pull-based crawling.
findings.recommendation: Add an IndexNow key and push new/changed product URLs on publish.

categories.name: Sitemap
what_works: Valid flat urlset, 229 URLs, exact expected count (212 products + 8 categories + 5 static/blog-index + 4 blog posts)
No duplicates, /cart and /search correctly excluded, referenced correctly in robots.txt
Spot-checked 18 URLs across every page type — all 200

findings.title: lastmod missing on 225/229 URLs
findings.description: Only the 4 blog posts have real lastmod dates.
findings.recommendation: Extend real-date lastmod logic to products/categories/static pages.

findings.title: priority/changefreq present but ignored by Google
findings.description: Harmless but safe to strip for a smaller file.
findings.recommendation: Optional cleanup.

categories.name: Content Quality & E-E-A-T
what_works: Rewritten product descriptions are specific, technical, and accurate — reads like it was written by someone who sells this equipment
Blog posts are practical and non-fluffy (properly hedges tax advice, gives real deposit figures)
No factual errors spotted in the sample

findings.title: Rewritten descriptions were ~65% invisible to crawlers
findings.description: ExpandableText sliced text to 480 chars before render; the rest only existed in the client hydration payload. FIXED during this audit — full text now always renders in the DOM with a CSS line-clamp for the visual toggle.
findings.recommendation: Resolved — verify re-indexing picks up full description text.

findings.title: About/Contact pages carry almost no trust signal
findings.description: About is ~90 words (no history, credentials, address); Contact is ~60 words (no street address).
findings.recommendation: Add a real About narrative and a verifiable physical address plus GBP link.

findings.title: All 4 blog posts are 10-15% of the 1,500-word floor, no bylines
findings.description: 150-190 words each, no author name/credential anywhere.
findings.recommendation: Expand posts with concrete detail; add named, credentialed bylines.

findings.title: Category pages have no unique topical copy
findings.description: Single tagline per category page, no differentiation.
findings.recommendation: Add 150-300 word intros per category.

findings.title: Repeated boilerplate CTA sentence across product bodies
findings.description: "Contact our team to confirm current availability" (or a close variant) closes many descriptions verbatim at scale.
findings.recommendation: Vary or remove; the Quote/Reserve buttons already cover that action.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: Organization schema present site-wide with ContactPoint
BreadcrumbList correct on every product/category page
Product/Offer schema well-formed when triggered, all 212 products carry a numeric price

findings.title: Product JSON-LD image URLs were malformed on all 212 products
findings.description: SITE.url was unconditionally prepended to already-absolute image URLs. FIXED during this audit.
findings.recommendation: Resolved.

findings.title: availability hardcoded to InStock regardless of real stock
findings.description: No stockStatus field drives this; a sold-out item would still declare InStock.
findings.recommendation: Add a stock field to the catalog pipeline and map accordingly.

findings.title: Offer block silently omitted if a no-price SKU appears
findings.description: Not currently triggered (all 212 have prices) but latent — a future no-price product loses Product rich-result eligibility entirely.
findings.recommendation: Handle the no-price case explicitly before it occurs.

findings.title: Organization schema missing sameAs
findings.description: No linked social/profile URLs.
findings.recommendation: Add once real business profiles exist.

findings.title: CollectionPage has no ItemList
findings.description: Category schema doesn't link to the products shown.
findings.recommendation: Add mainEntity ItemList referencing product URLs.

categories.name: Performance (Core Web Vitals)
categories.status: pending — specialist audit still running at report time, will be appended

categories.name: Visual / Mobile UX
categories.score_note: Measured before this audit's fixes shipped. The two Critical findings below (both image-related) are now resolved; remaining findings are still open.
what_works: Strong above-the-fold hero and clear CTAs on desktop and mobile
Dark theme with orange accent reads clean and on-brand, no broken grid at either width
Mobile nav collapses correctly, no horizontal scroll anywhere tested
Confirmed the earlier-suspected header/hero overlap is not a real bug (screenshot-stitching artifact only)

findings.title: Every product image broken (Vercel 402)
findings.description: Image optimizer returned 402 Payment Required for every remote image. FIXED during this audit (images.unoptimized).
findings.recommendation: Resolved — verify visually post-deploy.

findings.title: Product gallery unusable as a direct consequence
findings.description: Same root cause as above.
findings.recommendation: Resolved.

findings.title: "Add to Cart" tap target undersized on mobile category cards
findings.description: Measured 147x29px, under the 48px minimum touch-target guidance.
findings.recommendation: Increase min-height to 44-48px on mobile.

findings.title: "Request a Quote" button sits exactly at minimum tap-target size
findings.description: 48px on mobile, no comfortable margin next to an adjacent 50px button.
findings.recommendation: Bump both to ~52-56px with more gap.

findings.title: Category filter chips push inventory ~1.5 screens down on mobile
findings.description: 13 stacked filter chips before the first product card.
findings.recommendation: Collapse into an expandable filter drawer on mobile.

findings.title: Location formatting artifact ("Graham_TX")
findings.description: Traced to a bogus warehouse-location code sitting in the sku field on 44 products, not a location field. FIXED during this audit (nulled at the build step).
findings.recommendation: Resolved.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: robots.txt fully permits all major AI crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended)
Full SSR — raw curl returns complete product copy and JSON-LD, no headless rendering needed
Rewritten descriptions lead with concrete, source-near-subject facts in declarative sentences
Spec sections use semantic dl/dt/dd markup, easy for extraction pipelines

findings.title: llms.txt missing (404)
findings.description: No machine-readable index of key pages for LLM crawlers.
findings.recommendation: Publish /llms.txt listing top category/product URLs and policy summaries. ~1-2 hrs.

findings.title: No FAQ content or FAQPage schema anywhere
findings.description: Zero question-form headings site-wide — the single most-cited content shape for AI Overviews/ChatGPT direct answers.
findings.recommendation: Add a 3-5 item FAQ block per product template with FAQPage JSON-LD.

findings.title: Long undifferentiated paragraphs reduce single-passage citability
findings.description: Multi-fact paragraphs increase paraphrase/misattribution risk vs. one self-contained passage per fact.
findings.recommendation: Restructure into short, single-topic sub-paragraphs or bolded mini-headers.

findings.title: No comparison tables across similar SKUs
findings.description: 212 products, no visible size/model comparison tables despite being a natural fit for long-tail buying queries.
findings.recommendation: Add a small comparison table per product family.

findings.title: Weak off-site entity/brand signals
findings.description: No Wikipedia/Reddit/YouTube presence found; Organization schema has no sameAs.
findings.recommendation: Add sameAs links once real profiles exist; longer-term content plays.

findings.title: No last-verified date on spec data
findings.description: No freshness signal for currency-sensitive specs (e.g. emissions compliance).
findings.recommendation: Add a small 'specs verified as of' line.

categories.name: E-commerce / Product Data
categories.score_note: The Critical finding below is the same JSON-LD image bug already fixed under Schema — resolved.
what_works: Full SSG — all 212 products and 8 categories in raw HTML
Product/Organization/Breadcrumb JSON-LD with a populated Offer on all 212 products
Grade/type sub-filtering (?sub=) is server-rendered and canonicalizes correctly, avoiding facet duplicate-content bloat
Some condition/grade variants get distinct keyword-rich slugs (good long-tail pattern)

findings.title: Product schema image URLs broken
findings.description: Same bug as the Schema category finding.
findings.recommendation: Resolved.

findings.title: No self-hosted product images
findings.description: All images hotlink conexdepotshipping.com / cdn.shopify.com — dependency risk, blocks WebP/AVIF control, undermines Merchant Center origin-trust.
findings.recommendation: Mirror to owned storage/CDN with unique filenames (user has confirmed rights to the source imagery; self-hosting is the reliability/SEO fix regardless).

findings.title: No GTIN/MPN/SKU in Product schema
findings.description: sku is null for most products; no gtin/mpn/identifier_exists emitted — blocks Merchant Center feed acceptance.
findings.recommendation: Populate real internal SKUs or emit identifier_exists: false.

findings.title: New/Used condition variants collapsed into one page/price
findings.description: 94 of 212 products are type: variable with condition flattened into a text spec instead of distinct crawlable variants, inconsistent with how grade variants are handled elsewhere in the same catalog.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on distinct slugs/Offers per condition-grade combination.

findings.title: No combined facet filtering on category pages
findings.description: Only pre-built sub= grouping links, no cross-filter by size+condition+grade.
findings.recommendation: UX/conversion improvement, not a crawlability issue.

diagnostic_not_scored.name: Search Experience (SXO) — why aren't we ranking
diagnostic_not_scored.note: Not folded into the Health Score — this is a ranking-cause diagnostic, not a site-health metric.
key_findings: Critical: product copy is structurally similar to the scraped source network (conexoffcoast.com, longbeachoffcoastdepot.com, etc.) already occupying these SERPs with the same template pattern.
Critical: product images are hotlinked from conexdepotshipping.com — reinforces the duplicate-content signal and undermines originality trust.
High: 10ft-refrigerated-container-10ft-freezer and 10ft-refrigerated-containers-10ft-freezer cannibalize the same query — merge needed.
High: no cross-link between genset/reefer PDPs and the existing buying-guide blog content that would serve the researching buyer.
Medium: brand-name collision with containerone.net, an established BBB-accredited competitor with a near-identical name already ranking on shared target queries.

diagnostic_not_scored.name: Backlinks / Off-Page Authority
diagnostic_not_scored.note: Expected and not a fixable defect for a ~2-week-old rebrand — shown for context, not folded into the Health Score.
key_findings: Zero Common Crawl or Wayback captures at any date — no discoverable link/crawl footprint yet.
Realistic near-term tier: industry/equipment directories + genuine OEM dealer citations (Carrier/Thermo King/CAT/Kubota already referenced extensively in catalog data).
Do not attempt head-to-head link competition against 10+ year incumbents yet; re-baseline in 60-90 days.

fixes_applied_during_audit: next.config.ts: added images.remotePatterns for cdn.shopify.com/conexdepotshipping.com (was causing 500s on every page using next/image)
next.config.ts: added images.unoptimized to bypass Vercel's image optimizer, which was returning 402 Payment Required for every remote product photo
src/app/product/[slug]/page.tsx: fixed Product JSON-LD image URL concatenation bug affecting all 212 products
src/components/ExpandableText.tsx: full description text now always server-rendered; visual truncation moved to CSS line-clamp so crawlers see complete copy
src/app/product/[slug]/page.tsx: removed the rawCategories chip row (raw scraped marketplace taxonomy strings shown as tags)
scraper/scripts/build-catalog.js: cleared 44 products' bogus sku values (warehouse-location codes like "Graham_TX" mistakenly scraped as SKU)

scraper/scripts/build-catalog.js + src/lib/catalog.ts: removed sourceUrl/siteName fields from the compiled, public products.json (unused by the app, but exposed literal competitor page URLs for all 212 products in this public repo)
Untracked scraper/output, config.json, README.md, description-rewrite-brief.md from the public repo (named source sites/competitors); kept locally, added to .gitignore
